files.00_Overview.md: Expand to include proper Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways sections. The current content is a good quick reference but lacks the 3W framework structure (What/Why/How). Add a visual learning roadmap showing the progression through files 01-07.
files.01_Introduction.md: Excellent foundation with comprehensive 3W framework and OpenFOAM Context boxes. Consider condensing the theoretical comparisons with std::shared_ptr - this content overlaps with file 04. Focus on practical CFD workflows and memory challenges specific to OpenFOAM users.
files.02_Memory_Syntax_and_Design.md: Well-structured with practical examples. Missing Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways sections. The code examples are good but would benefit from 'Anti-Pattern vs Best Practice' comparisons showing common mistakes. Add a section on when to choose each smart pointer type.
files.03_Internal_Mechanics.md: Good technical depth with implementation details. Lacks proper 3W framework - jumps straight into code without explaining WHY these mechanisms matter to CFD practitioners. Add performance considerations and cache behavior discussion. Missing Key Takeaways summary.
files.04_Mathematical_Foundations.md: Outstanding mathematical rigor with proper state machine diagrams and OpenFOAM Context integration. The cache alignment and false sharing sections are excellent. However, this is highly theoretical - consider adding a 'Practical Implications' section showing how to apply this knowledge when debugging memory issues.
files.05_Implementation_Mechanisms.md: Shows significant redundancy with file 03 - both cover tmp implementation, reference counting, and RAII. Merge the unique content from this file (RAII patterns, ownership transfer) into file 03 and remove this file OR refocus it on 'Code-Level Implementation Patterns' with advanced techniques.
files.06_Design_Patterns_and_Add: Good coverage of RAII, Factory, and Registry patterns. Missing Learning Objectives. Add a 'Pattern Selection Guide' - flowchart helping users choose the right pattern for their use case. The Object Registry pattern needs expansion with real OpenFOAM lookupObject examples.
files.07_Common_Errors_and_Debugging.md: Practical and well-structured with problem-solution format. Excellent for troubleshooting. Missing Key Takeaways section. Add a 'Debugging Checklist' and 'Memory Profiling Tools' section (e.g., valgrind, sanitizers). Could expand with more OpenFOAM-specific error messages.

global_strategy: This folder has a solid foundation but suffers from content overlap (especially between files 03 and 05) and inconsistent application of the 3W framework. RECOMMENDED ACTIONS: 1) Consolidate files 03 and 05 to eliminate redundancy - merge implementation mechanisms into Internal_Mechanics.md and repurpose the freed slot for '08_Performance_Optimization.md' covering cache behavior, profiling, and memory tuning; 2) Standardize all files to include: Learning Objectives (at start), 3W structure throughout, and Key Takeaways (at end); 3) Add cross-reference links showing how theoretical concepts in files 01-04 connect to practical patterns in files 05-07; 4) Create a visual dependency graph showing prerequisite relationships between files. The bilingual approach (Thai/English) is maintained well and should continue.
